description: Use this API to add or modify all OPF generator reactive capability records in a specified subsystem of the working case. This API routine was first introduced in release 28.0.0. ierr = opf_gen_rcap_subsys(sid, all, rcstat, realar, useval)
python_syntax: ierr = opf_gen_rcap_subsys(sid, all, rcstat, realar, useval)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: A valid subsystem id from 0 to 11. a subsystem must have been previously defined. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: All buses or specified subsystem flag. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: rcstat
parameters.description: Reactive capability limit status (default value of 4 for new records, current value otherwise). (input).

parameters.name: realar
parameters.description: An array of five elements specifying The value of each is as follows. (input).

parameters.name: useval
parameters.description: An array of five elements specifying (default treatment same as value of 0). (input).

rcstat.description: in service, no reactive generation limits.
in service, apply reactive generation limits.

realar.description: direct-axis synchronous reactance in pu on Mbase (default value of 1.0 for new records, current value otherwise).
stator current limit in pu on Mbase (default value of 1.0 for new records, current value otherwise).
rated lagging power factor (default value of 1.0 for new records, current value otherwise).
rated leading power factor (default value of 1.0 for new records, current value otherwise).
maximum reactive absorption in pu on Mbase (default value of 1.0 for new records, current value otherwise).

useval.description: indicates how the value of REALAR(1) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(1) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(1) is to be multiplied by the present value of the direct-axis synchronous reactance.
if the value of REALAR(1) is to be added to the present value of the direct- axis synchronous reactance.
indicates how the value of REALAR(2) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(2) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(2) is to be multiplied by the present value of the stator current limit.
if the value of REALAR(2) is to be added to the present value of the stator current limit.
indicates how the value of REALAR(3) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(3) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(3) is to be multiplied by the present value of the rated lagging power factor.
if the value of REALAR(3) is to be added to the present value of the rated lagging power factor.
indicates how the value of REALAR(4) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(4) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(4) is to be multiplied by the present value of the rated leading power factor.
if the value of REALAR(4) is to be added to the present value of the rated leading power factor.
indicates how the value of REALAR(5) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(5) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(5) is to be multiplied by the present value of the maximum reactive absorption.
if the value of REALAR(5) is to be added to the present value of the maximum reactive absorption.

error_codes.description: invalid SID value or subsystem SID is not defined.
generator reactive capability data table is full.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
non-fatal error occurred. Possible warning messages are: - Invalid reactive capability status specified.
